capabilities: Use property-based and contract testing to validate LLM outputs, focusing on structural, semantic, and factual properties to maintain reliability during model updates.

procedure.action: Specify output properties using JSON Schema to capture expected structural and semantic attributes.

Invoke Hypothesis to generate diverse prompt variants that test defined properties across LLM outputs by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Run semantic similarity checks with Sentence Transformers to ensure outputs meet semantic expectations using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Establish consumer-driven contracts using Pact to define and verify expected interactions with LLM outputs.

Compose contract tests into CI/CD pipelines to automatically validate outputs against defined contracts by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.

summary: Adapt property-based and contract testing to LLM outputs by focusing on output properties rather than exact matches, ensuring robustness across prompt variations and model changes.
title: Applying Property-Based and Contract Testing to LLM Outputs

failureConditions: Silent regressions occur during model updates; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Outputs fail to meet structural expectations; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Semantic drift in outputs across prompt variations; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
successConditions: All generated prompt variants pass property-based tests
Contract tests pass in CI/CD pipeline with no failures
Semantic similarity scores exceed 0.8 for key outputs
